## [00:00 - 03:01]

Today we go to Lake Louise, we've got all our camera gear, we've got the drone, it's going to be a great day. Good old Valley Village, get us some boots and gloves. Might be a little bit big for me Craig. Right on, got our beanies, got our second hand boots, we're ready for the snow. You know being out here in the mountains, it reminds me of this really awesome little story that I'm sure I'm going to butcher but I'm going to tell you anyway. So there's once a group of frogs in the jungle and there was a myth that whichever frog could get to the top of the mountain will be king of the jungle. I know it would be ridiculous and ludicrous that a frog could be king of the jungle but so now all the frogs go on this massive mission and they try to get to the top of the mountain and then the first group of frogs, all these frogs are gone, thousands of them and then they pass the first part of the mountain, they get to the first little summit right and then heaps of the frogs are like man this is crazy, what the hell are we doing, we're fricking frogs right so a whole bunch of them leave and turn back and go home. But then I've still a bunch of them charge on and keep going and then when they get to the next part it gets really steep now, the little frog legs are killing them but they're jumping along and they're cruising along and they're going to go past the like ferocious river as well and they get past the river and a whole bunch of them goes guys seriously what are we thinking man, we're just frogs, it's stupid, this is stupid so then a whole bunch of them turn back and go as well. And then only a small handful now keep charging along and they just keep going, they keep climbing, they keep climbing, long story short in the end one frog makes it, right one frog and the bunch of the other animals that are up on top of the mountain are like dude you've made it, this is crazy, how tell us you know all the pain and the torture you've been through what was it like getting to the top of the mountain. The frog just kind of sits there and doesn't say anything and everyone's like okay, okay well tell us, come on he's probably in shock the poor frog, he's gotten so high, well tell us you know it doesn't get enough oxygen, tell us like what was it like and the frog just looks at them and it doesn't say anything and it just blows their mind they're like right he's just an arrogant bastard of a frog right and then in the end they worked out. This frog was actually deaf and the crazy part of the story is I just believe in all of our lives that we times when we're trying to climb mountains like that in our life and there were dreams that we have that seem like they're on top of mountains like that and there were times when we have to block our ears, there were times when we have to just stop ourselves from listening to the negativity that's out there in the world and be like that deaf frog. People are going to say things, people are going to tell you that you're not going to be able to do it, you don't have enough talent, you don't have enough skill, you're not beautiful enough, you're not perfect enough to make it on the journey but you need to block those you need to block those people out. We finally arrived at Lake Louise and it's just whiteness, there's nothing else to it but it just goes to show that sometimes in life the journey to get to the destination is more fun than the destination itself. It's almost, it's kind of like that frog story. If someone at the beginning of our journey told us that this is what we were going to see, guess what? We probably wouldn't have even gone on the journey but because we had somewhat deaf

## [03:01 - 03:16]

ears, we went along anyway, we had an incredible time on the way. So it's a metaphor for life. There are going to be some journeys that you go on where the destination will be like this and you know what? The journey is going to turn out a lot better than the destination so go on the journey.
